perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party may disclose the Confidential I nformation only to employees or 2 consultants of the Receiving Party to have a “need to know” the Confidential Information in order to enable the Receiving Party to use the Conf idential Information for the purpose intended under this Agreement and are legally bound not to use or disclose the Confidential Information for any other purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party may disclose the Confidential I nformation to employees, consultants, or advisors of the Receiving Party, regardless of a “need to know.” Disclosure to any such party constitutes acceptance of full responsibility for the protection and nondisclosure of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a conflict by removing the 'need to know' restriction. Section 2 initially limits disclosure to those employees and consultants with a 'need to know,' but the changed text allows disclosure to a broader range of individuals without this condition. This in-text contradiction creates uncertainty about who can access the information and the extent of the Receiving Party's responsibility.
perturbation.contradicted_text: NON -DISCLOSURE . The Receiving Party acknowledges that the Confid ential Information contains valuable trade and technical secrets of th e Disclosing Party. The Receiving Party shall not copy (in whole or in part), sell, assign, lease, license, disclose, give or otherwise transfer the Confidential Information or any copy t hereof to any third party or otherwise use the Confidential Information other than for the purpose intended under this Agreement. The Receiving Party may disclose the Confidential I nformation to employees, consultants, or advisors of the Receiving Party, regardless of a “need to know.” Disclosure to any such party constitutes acceptance of full responsibility for the protection and nondisclosure of the Confidential Information. The Receiving P arty shall treat the Confidential Information with the same degree of confidentiality as it keeps its own confidential information, but in all events no less than a reaso nable degree of confidentiality. This obligation shall be binding on all employees, offic ers, subsidiaries, affiliates or successors of the Receiving Party and shall continue for a period of five years from the date that the Confidential Information is provided to the Receivi ng Party, regardless of whether the Agreement has been terminated. The Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information if so required by judicial or administrative proces s, provided that it shall promptly notify the Disclosing Party so as to allow the Disclosing Part y reasonable time to oppose such process, and provided that Receiving Party shall minimize su ch disclosure to the extent possible.
perturbation.justification: The original text limited disclosure of confidential information to employees or consultants with a 'need to know.' The changed text removes this 'need to know' restriction, allowing disclosure to a broader range of individuals. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, which confirms this expanded disclosure possibility within the non-disclosure section, but this expansion contradicts the initial 'need to know' limitation, creating a clear in-text contradiction as defined under 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: This obligation shall be binding on all employees, offic ers, subsidiaries, affiliates or successors of the Receiving Party and shall continue for a period of five years from the date that the Confidential Information is provided to the Receivi ng Party, regardless of whether the Agreement has been terminated.
perturbation.changed_text: This obligation shall be binding on all employees, officers, subsidiaries, affiliates or successors of the Receiving Party, and shall cease immediately upon the termination of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a direct contradiction regarding the duration of the non-disclosure obligation. The original text states that the obligation continues for five years even after termination of the agreement. The modified text states that it ceases immediately upon termination. This creates significant uncertainty about the enforceability of the non-disclosure agreement after termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: NON -DISCLOSURE . The Receiving Party acknowledges that the Confid ential Information contains valuable trade and technical secrets of th e Disclosing Party. The Receiving Party shall not copy (in whole or in part), sell, assign, lease, license, disclose, give or otherwise transfer the Confidential Information or any copy t hereof to any third party or otherwise use the Confidential Information other than for the purpose intended under this Agreement. The Receiving Party may disclose the Confidential I nformation to employees, consultants, or advisors of the Receiving Party, regardless of a “need to know.” Disclosure to any such party constitutes acceptance of full responsibility for the protection and nondisclosure of the Confidential Information. The Receiving P arty shall treat the Confidential Information with the same degree of confidentiality as it keeps its own confidential information, but in all events no less than a reaso nable degree of confidentiality. This obligation shall be binding on all employees, officers, subsidiaries, affiliates or successors of the Receiving Party, and shall cease immediately upon the termination of this agreement. The Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information if so required by judicial or administrative proces s, provided that it shall promptly notify the Disclosing Party so as to allow the Disclosing Part y reasonable time to oppose such process, and provided that Receiving Party shall minimize su ch disclosure to the extent possible.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction with the contradicted text regarding the duration of the non-disclosure obligation. The original text states that the obligation continues for five years even after termination of the agreement, while the modified text (which is also present in the contradicted text) states that it ceases immediately upon termination. This creates significant uncertainty about the enforceability of the non-disclosure agreement after termination, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: this Agreement may only be amended by a writing of subsequent date that is signed by both parties.
perturbation.changed_text: this Agreement may be amended by email correspondence between authorized representatives of both parties.
perturbation.explanation: The original contract clearly specifies the amendment process: a written agreement signed by both parties. The revised text states that the contract can be amended by email. This is a direct contradiction on how the contract can be legally amended.
perturbation.contradicted_text: M ISCELLANEOUS . Either party may assign or otherwise transfer any of its rights or obligations under this Agreement to any third party, provided that they inform the other party within 30 days of the transfer; failure by a party to enforce any provisions of this Agreement at any time 3 shall in no manner affect the right of that party a t a later time to enforce any provision of this Agreement; this Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of England without reference to the principles of conflict of laws; in the event that any word, phrase, clause, sentence or other provision h erein shall violate any applicable statute, ordinance or rule of law in any jurisdiction which governs this Agreement, such provisions shall be effective to the extent of such violation without invalidating any other provision herein; this Agreement supersedes all previous unde rstandings or agreements between the parties and incorporates the entire agreement of th e parties with respect to the receipt and use of the Confidential Information; this Agreement may be amended by email correspondence between authorized representatives of both parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that amendments must be in writing and signed by both parties. The changed text allows amendments via email correspondence. The contradicted text contains the changed text, 'this Agreement may be amended by email correspondence between authorized representatives of both parties,' which directly contradicts the original intent of formal written and signed amendments, creating a clear inconsistency within the contract, leading to a 'YES'.
